title: Episode 04 Blood on the Wall
wikitext:
[]: Episode 04: Blood on the Wall             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Jan 01, 2010 7:00 pm After Shinji is recovered from his escape-scapade, Shinji is, as the Evacommentary project put it, "made to sit inside a dark room. The red mark of Nerv is on the bare concrete wall. Light flows in as the door opens." That's fine and all, but- wuuuuuuuh? A large amount of what seems to be blood is splattered on the wall. Only one sentence of thought in the commentary project is given to this: Dr. Nick wrote: Are those blood stains on the wall? "Throw him into the torture chamber!"

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Jan 01, 2010 7:02 pm rust

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Jan 01, 2010 7:09 pm The stain shows much more prominently in the light than the rest of the wall. Rust doesn't do that. Also, does concrete rust?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Jan 01, 2010 7:11 pm it's still rust heavy, dark rust. The kind you see in movies. That kind of rust, movie rust

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Fri Jan 01, 2010 7:12 pm Xard wrote: rust

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Jan 01, 2010 7:13 pm It could just be an affect of the light, via depressions and elevations in the concrete. I've never really heard a good explanation for these, but I'd be skeptical of a bloodstained torture chamber. Perhaps they are stains from fumes of some kind?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jan 01, 2010 7:13 pm Dry blood isn't red.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jan 01, 2010 7:14 pm That's all from Shinji. ...he fell down some stairs. Nah, it's probably just paint. The stencil slipped or something.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Jan 01, 2010 7:14 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Dry blood isn't red.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jan 01, 2010 7:17 pm ...or perhaps it's what's left of the other prisoner they put psycho boy in here with.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jan 01, 2010 7:22 pm ...I've always thought this shot was creepy. Either way, I doubt Shinji himself was somehow tortured, there would have been something implicitly or explicitly stating such if so.

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Fri Jan 01, 2010 7:30 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Dry blood isn't red.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Jan 01, 2010 7:30 pm It may be a residue leak of bakelite behind the walls. Based on EoE, it would seem the bakelite pipes run throughout the entirety of Nerv HQ.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Fri Jan 01, 2010 9:36 pm "The walls in the 53rd precinct were bleeding. How do you explain that ?"

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Jan 01, 2010 9:47 pm Clearly, there's only one explanation for this. NERV HQ is an Eva. Yeah. You heard me. The pyramid is the peaked helmet of a giant god-damned Eva, the ultimate model, buried in the Geofront by Gendou as a weapon of last resort should Adam itself ever awaken. It is capable of gattai with Unit 01 to create Arc Unit 01. If the two should combine, shit fucking explodes everywhere forever. Or maybe it's just rust or mold or something.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Jan 02, 2010 12:36 am Ironfoot wrote: AuraTwilight wrote: Dry blood isn't red.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Jan 02, 2010 1:04 am AuraTwilight wrote: Awesome. Cool thing those walls aren't concrete, huh? They're metal.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Jan 02, 2010 5:23 am Hmmm, more illogical "red" showing up solely to reiterate the association of red = harsh reality? Possibly. I always assumed it was rust and blood doesn't really seem to make sense. I can't for the life of me figure out what the hell matter it makes.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Jan 02, 2010 5:40 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Perhaps they are stains from fumes of some kind?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Jan 02, 2010 5:44 am Ornette wrote: AuraTwilight wrote: Awesome. Cool thing those walls aren't concrete, huh? They're metal.
